title: Illumina PCR-Free library preparation
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"6drog","text":"This protocol describes the library preparation for Illumina sequencing. It accompanies the GigaScience publication:","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"italic","offset":92,"length":11}],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"9mpp1","text":" ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"9aboc","text":"Benjamin Istace, et al. (2017) De novo assembly and population genomic survey of natural yeast isolates with the Oxford Nanopore MinION sequencer. steps: Fragment the DNA (6µg): 100 to 1500bp size using a Covaris E210 sonicator
DNA fragments were End-repaired by using NEBNext® End Repair Module
The end-repair reaction was cleaned up with Agencourt AMPure XP beads (1x)
DNA fragments were dA-tailed by using the NEBNext® dA-Tailing Module
The dA-tail reaction was cleaned up with AMPure beads (1x)
Adaptors were ligated to the dA-tailed DNA fragment using NEBNext® Ligation Module
The ligation reaction was cleaned up with AMPure beads (1x)
The ligation reaction was cleaned up with AMPure beads (0,6x)
The library was quantified by qPCR by using the KAPA Library Quantification Kit for Illumina Libraries
The library profile was assessed by using a DNA High Sensitivity LabChip kit and an Agilent Bioanalyzer
creator: Gigascience Database
